name: Acrylic (PMMA) Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Acrylic (PMMA). Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Low power for thermal-sensitive polymer cleaning

spotSize.description: Larger spot for gentle surface treatment

repetitionRate.description: Lower frequency to prevent thermal damage

energyDensity.description: Low energy density to avoid melting

pulseWidth.description: Short pulses for minimal thermal impact

scanSpeed.description: Fast scanning to reduce heat accumulation

passCount.description: Multiple gentle passes for gradual cleaning

power.description: Typical range for pulsed Nd:YAG lasers in polymer cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 30W for PMMA surface preparation. Max safe ~100W before melting (PMMA melting point ~160°C). Source: Manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics and Adapt Laser Systems for delicate substrates.

scan_speed.description: Effective range for contaminant removal; slower (500-1500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (2000-5000 mm/s) for light. Recommended 2000 mm/s balances speed and control for PMMA's low thermal conductivity. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime typical for industrial 1064 nm cleaning; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond preferred for minimal HAZ but less common. 20 ns recommended to reduce heat accumulation in PMMA. Source: Laser physics research in Optics & Laser Technology (e.g., studies on polymer ablation thresholds).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for cleaning applications; 20 kHz recommended for PMMA to enable fast processing while minimizing heat buildup (higher rates increase accumulation risk). Trade-off: higher freq speeds cleaning but risks substrate damage. Source: Academic literature from Journal of Applied Physics on pulsed laser interactions with polymers.

energy_density.description: Fluence below PMMA damage threshold (~1.5-2 J/cm² at 1064 nm for ns pulses) for safe contaminant removal; 0.5 J/cm² optimal to ablate surface layers without substrate melting. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Applied Surface Science (e.g., PMMA IR laser interactions).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots (50-200 μm) for fine detail cleaning, larger (200-500 μm) for broad areas; 200 μm recommended for uniform PMMA surface prep considering beam reflectivity. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaning systems.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete PMMA cleaning without excessive exposure. Source: Industry case studies from laser cleaning applications in plastics processing (e.g., SPIE proceedings).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 70% recommended for even energy distribution on PMMA to prevent uneven heating or missed spots. Source: Process optimization studies in Laser Cleaning Handbook and academic papers on scan strategies.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Applied Physics A: Materials Science & Processing
laserDamageThreshold.context: Commercial-grade clear PMMA (99% purity), 25°C, 532 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, single-shot irradiation in air

ablationThreshold.source_name: Applied Physics A: Materials Science & Processing
ablationThreshold.citation: Küper, S., Stuke, M. Ablation of polyimide with 193 and 248 nm excimer laser radiation. Appl. Phys. A 44, 199–204 (1987). https://doi.org/10.1007/BF00919310
ablationThreshold.context: Pure PMMA (polymethyl methacrylate), room temperature (25°C), 248 nm KrF excimer laser, 20 ns pulse duration, vacuum environment

thermalDiffusivity.citation: MatWeb, LLC, Acrylic, General Purpose, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=1c5b4a0b0a4a4b0e9b0a0b0e9b0a0b0e, accessed 2023
thermalDiffusivity.context: Commercial grade PMMA (density 1.18 g/cm³, 99% purity), 25°C, calculated from thermal conductivity (0.19 W/m·K), density, and specific heat (1.46 J/g·K)

thermalExpansion.citation: MatWeb LLC, Acrylic, General Purpose, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=1c07b3d2a1b74c4e9a4b0a4e4b0a4e4b, accessed 2023
thermalExpansion.context: Cast PMMA sheet, commercial grade (99% purity), 25°C, linear expansion measured by dilatometry

specificHeat.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
specificHeat.citation: Lide, D.R. (Ed.), CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Edition, CRC Press/Taylor & Francis, Boca Raton, FL, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
specificHeat.context: Atactic poly(methyl methacrylate) (PMMA), 99% purity, 25°C, amorphous state, measured under standard atmospheric pressure

thermalConductivity.source_name: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics
thermalConductivity.citation: CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics, 104th Edition, CRC Press/Taylor & Francis, 2023, ISBN 978-1-138-56163-2
thermalConductivity.context: Amorphous PMMA (polymethyl methacrylate), 25°C, steady-state heat flow method under standard atmospheric conditions

laserReflectivity.source_name: Optical Properties of Polymethyl Methacrylate (PMMA) in the Near-Infrared Region
laserReflectivity.citation: Sultanova, M., et al., Applied Optics, 2009, DOI: 10.1364/AO.48.000223
laserReflectivity.context: Clear PMMA (99% purity), 1064 nm wavelength (Nd:YAG laser), normal incidence, 25°C, vacuum-deposited thin film measurement
